title: rubus maassii subsp. mercicus
intro: Rubus maassii subsp. mercicus, also known as the Black Raspberry, is a type of bramble plant native to the north-eastern regions of North America. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus maassii subsp. mercicus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus maassii subsp. mercicus typically involve making jams, jellies, and infusions from the plant's leaves, roots, and fruit, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
